title: cryptogramma crispa var. brunoniana
intro: Cryptogramma crispa var. brunoniana, also known as the rock fern, is a type of plant species in the family Adiantaceae, native to Western North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The rock fern is used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the rock fern is prepared as a tea or infusion by drying and steeping the leaves in hot water, and it can also be used in tincture or salve form to harness its medicinal properties.
